text_en: Both morphemes attach directly to what follows; do not insert 的.
These suffixes form one lexical unit with the expression before them.
Put the reference place or object before the location noun.
The resulting location phrase can follow 在 or come before 有.
The modal comes before the action verb.
Negate the modal itself: 不会 or 不能.
Both can introduce an action; 要 can also take a noun as the wanted item.
Add 吗 to the sentence to ask politely whether an action is allowed.
At this level, learn each item as a common activity or state while recognizing its internal verb-object structure.
Replace the unknown sentence part with the matching interrogative pronoun.
Do not add 吗 to a question that already uses one of these question words.
A personal pronoun can serve as subject, object, or possessor according to its position.
这-series forms point near the speaker; 那-series forms point farther away or back to known information.
有的 and 有些 select only part of a larger set.
Build larger numbers from the highest place value to the lowest.
Classifier choice is lexical: learn the common noun-classifier pairing.
The container noun 杯 is borrowed to classify the amount it holds.
Place the number directly before the time unit; these units do not take an extra classifier.
Place the degree adverb before the adjective or adjective-like predicate.
很 is often natural before a simple adjective even when 'very' is not strongly stressed.
都 scopes over the set to its left, not a noun introduced later in the sentence.
正在 is more explicit and emphatic about the action being underway at the reference moment.
A time expression such as 明天 can clarify when the later repetition will happen.
Both depend on prior or understood information; the sentence presents something as additional rather than isolated.
Completed-event negation uses 没 without perfective 了.
Keep the 在 phrase before the main action in this beginner pattern.
Both prepositional phrases normally appear before the main predicate.
A and B should be grammatically parallel, such as two nouns or two noun phrases.
The modifier can be a pronoun, noun, or descriptive phrase.
Negate a nonoccurring completed event with 没 and normally remove perfective 了.
The syllabus indices distinguish functions; they are not pronounced and are not written in the Chinese sentence.
Intonation and context determine whether 喂 sounds neutral, urgent, or abrupt.
Nouns commonly use 和; compact paired expressions can occur directly side by side.
The head carries the phrase's basic category; the earlier material modifies it.
A verb-object phrase can function as the predicate or as part of a larger construction.
Identify the internal relationship first: who or what is the predicate about?
The numeral and measure word form a unit before the noun.
At HSK 1, the complete phrase normally precedes the main action.
The resulting phrase can follow 在 or serve as the place before 有.
The subject normally precedes the predicate in the neutral order.
Common HSK 1 nominal-predicate domains include dates, weekdays, age, and measured values.
Place either predicate type after the subject in the neutral simple sentence.
Identify the verb first, then ask what or whom it directly selects.
The attributive and head noun form one larger nominal phrase.
Order broad time before place, then place the most tightly linked adverb near its predicate.
In requests, 一下 can reduce the weight of the command without removing its action-measure meaning.
This is a sentence classification based on the predicate's grammatical type.
A bare adjective can sound contrastive; 很 often gives a neutral descriptive reading.
Use this construction only in established nominal-predicate domains at this level.
Weather statements, calls, and short situational expressions commonly use this form.
A declarative normally presents its content for the listener to accept as information.
No subject-verb inversion occurs.
A specific-information question normally does not take final 吗.
The answer can repeat the positive predicate or give its negative form.
The understood subject is often the person being addressed and need not be spoken.
Degree words and intonation commonly carry the speaker's evaluation.
Negate this pattern with 不是.
Use 没有 before the possessed item for the negative form.
This pattern identifies the entity at a known place; the following 有 pattern instead presents an existing quantity.
The entity after 有 is typically new information and often has an indefinite quantity.
Do not add a conjunction between the two verb phrases in this basic construction.
Transport expressions such as 坐出租车 and 开车 commonly supply the manner of 去 or 来.
Both objects belong to the same verb; no second 给 is needed in this basic pattern.
A natural time, cause, or event sequence lets the listener infer how the clauses connect.
The repeated or understood comparison creates the parallel reading.
还 often feels like 'and in addition,' so the second clause expands the information rather than merely matching it.
Use 没 without perfective 了 to deny that the event occurred.
Context supplies the earlier state against which the change is understood.
Progressive aspect is suited to ongoing activities, not every state or identity predicate.
正在 is the more emphatic preverbal choice; both variants can close with neutral-tone 呢.
This compact form is especially natural when answering what someone is doing or correcting an assumption.
A price can serve as a direct quantity predicate after the item being priced.
The tone of 一 remains first tone in the ordinal number 第一.
Read the digits of a year individually; read month and day numbers as ordinary cardinal numbers.
When the minute value is understood in ordinary speech, 分 may be omitted, but the full HSK 1 formula keeps it explicit.
The direction and 面 form a single location word, such as 里面 or 后面.
Add 边 when naming a relative location: 左边 or 右边.
AA, A一A, and ABAB commonly soften requests or suggest trying an action.
A了A reports that the brief action was actually carried out.
可能 normally comes before the verb or adjectival predicate it qualifies.
The four syllabus items are 帮忙, 游泳, 跳舞, and 跑步.
A classifier can enter the word, as in 帮一个忙.
Do not add 吗 to a question already formed with 为什么.
Before a verb, 自己 can emphasize doing something without another person's help.
Before 的 + noun, 自己 identifies something belonging to the relevant person.
这么 and 那么 commonly modify degree; 这样 and 那样 commonly point to a way of acting.
Use 的 when the reduplicated phrase describes a noun or forms a descriptive predicate; use 地 before an action.
万 is an exact place-value unit; 好多 and quantity 多 can leave the exact amount open.
Place the classifier between a number or demonstrative and its noun.
The noun naming a package is borrowed into the classifier position.
Use 两次, not 二次, for two occurrences in ordinary speech.
多 and 好 are expressive here; 最 makes a superlative comparison.
The participants may be a plural subject or be established by context.
有时 and 有时候 are interchangeable short and full forms in these examples.
正 commonly combines with 在 or final 呢, but it can also stand directly before the verb.
常 is shorter and can sound slightly more compact; 经常 is common in neutral speech.
The reviewed examples use 经常; 常 remains an explicitly listed member of the official grammar inventory.
Adding a subject can make the intended listener explicit; context often makes it unnecessary.
These indexed functions connect events; 再1 elsewhere can simply mean 'again.'
The syllabus numbers distinguish these discourse functions from earlier or connective uses of the same surface forms.
A complete range can use 从……到…… for its beginning and end.
Direction words such as 前, 左边, or 里面 can follow 往.
Directional complements such as 过来 or 过去 can make the movement perspective explicit.
Context and word order distinguish prepositional 给 from the independent verb 给 'to give.'
Do not add 是 before a simple adjectival result such as 高 or 冷.
一起 can be added when the meaning is explicitly joint participation.
The reason phrase stays integrated with the main subject and predicate.
At this level, use 跟 to join words or phrases, not two independent clauses.
但 is a shorter counterpart of 但是.
虽然 commonly anticipates a contrasting second clause, while 因为 commonly anticipates a result.
Their opposite positions around the verb reflect their different grammatical jobs.
In a negative experience statement, use 没 + Verb + 过.
The surrounding verb determines whether the continuation is understood mainly as a state or an action.
吧2 differs from suggestion 吧1 because it asks the listener to confirm an assumption.
Sentence-final 的2 does not modify a following noun.
Result complements and 得-complements are two HSK 2 examples of this broader structural category.
The complement follows the verb it completes or describes.
The modifier may describe a property, possessor, or action associated with the understood noun.
The first action may enable or provide the setting for the second.
Common HSK 2 first verbs in this pattern include 请, 叫, and 让.
Nominal phrases can identify people or things and can occupy subject or object position.
Verbal phrases range from a verb-object unit to a longer sequence with time or aspect material.
An adjectival phrase may be a predicate or may modify a noun with 的.
The expression is common in informal speech and keeps the remaining items deliberately vague.
The construction often implies that another option was considered and set aside.
快要 emphasizes closeness; 就要 often adds a sense of immediacy or a known schedule.
The pattern often prepares a reaction to the lateness or extent of the situation.
This word order is natural when the affected item is already known or is the main topic.
Time and age expressions are especially common nominal predicates at this level.
A verbal modifier has no expressed internal subject; a subject-predicate modifier includes its own subject.
Choose the complement for the result: error, comprehension, readiness, acquired skill, or completion.
The same physical action can select 来 or 去 when the conversational viewpoint changes.
The six complements encode distinct paths; later compounds add 来 or 去 for viewpoint.
起来 lexicalizes the upward movement without a contrasting 去 form in this inventory.
With an object, a common order is Object + Verb + 得 + Description, as in 汉语说得很好.
Simple intransitive actions can take the duration directly after the verb.
Age, price, and small degree differences commonly appear in this structure at HSK 2.
Body-part descriptions are common: the person is the topic and the body part is the internal subject.
Repeat the verb when the alternatives involve different complete actions.
Tone and context determine whether the sentence sounds like advice, a warning, or a firm command.
A color phrase ending in 的 can serve as the descriptive expression after 是.
The pattern commonly describes an activity that began earlier and remains relevant now.
The post-verbal noun phrase is often indefinite because the sentence introduces it into the discourse.
A and B should be comparable in the property named by the adjective.
Use an exact measure such as 两岁 or an approximate degree such as 一点儿.
这么 and 那么 make the referenced degree more explicit; context guides the deictic choice.
Keep 得 immediately after the performance verb in either variant.
The focused material can be time, place, manner, or the event's doer.
Do not use this focus frame for an event that is merely planned and not yet realized.
请 is polite, 叫 commonly conveys telling, and 让 can mean either allow or cause depending on context.
This order differs from prepositional 给 + Person placed before an unrelated main verb.
The initial 是 can frame the contrast but is not required when the alternatives are already clear.
但 may replace 但是 in a more compact version, but the full paired format is the target here.
In context one connector may sometimes be omitted, but the full paired construction displays both roles clearly.
The construction can describe a specific immediate sequence or a regular whenever-then relationship.
Open, held, worn, and seated postures commonly receive this state reading.
This function emphasizes the continuing activity rather than a resulting posture.
Negate an absent experience as 没 + Verb + 过.
Floors and calendar dates commonly omit 第 in ordinary expressions.
Aspect and sentence context distinguish an approximate statement from an information question.
The base is normally a round value such as 十 or 二十.
Time quantities such as 一个多小时 and 两年多 commonly use this order.
Use 老 only with conventional or contextually natural human labels.
Learn the resulting forms as whole words while recognizing the contribution of the suffix.
Single direction nouns and 方 forms can identify an area; 中间 locates something within or between references.
These modals normally stand before the action they qualify; 需要 can also take a noun object.
应该 gives advice or an expected duty, while 得 often presents the necessity as immediate and unavoidable.
At this level, use each form naturally as an activity while recognizing its internal verb-object structure.
离开 commonly takes the place left, 完成 takes the task completed, and 分开 describes separation.
The answer replaces 怎样 with the requested manner or description.
Translate according to the question word: 谁都 is 'everyone/anyone,' 什么都 is 'everything/anything,' and 哪儿都 is 'everywhere/anywhere.'
Words such as 想, 好像, or an uncertain context often support the indefinite reading.
Both forms can occupy ordinary noun-phrase positions as subject, object, or possessor.
Both can modify a following noun; 别的 can also stand alone when the noun is understood.
Place the selected measure word between the numeral or demonstrative and the noun.
Weight, money, time, and length measures function as units after numbers, while classifiers such as 张 and 辆 select noun types.
Put 碗 or 盘 after the numeral and before the food.
Place the numeral and verbal measure after the action, with any object positioned according to the verb's normal pattern.
个个 is broadly useful for people or things; 张张 distributes over items counted with 张.
Most members stand before the adjective or mental-state verb; 极 also appears in the complement 极了.
Place the scope adverb before the expression it limits or quantifies.
These adverbs normally appear before the verb; 先 can pair with 再 or 然后 to order actions.
Place the frequency adverb before the repeated predicate.
When the subject is shared, state it once before the first 一边.
Place these adverbs before the predicate or quantity whose certainty, necessity, or approximation they qualify.
These forms organize the speaker's attitude toward the proposition rather than simply locating it in time.
不必 can sound slightly more formal; 不用 is frequent in everyday reassurance and replies.
All three prepositional phrases normally appear before the main predicate.
Common direction objects include 左, 右, 前, 后, and place nouns.
The purpose or reason phrase can precede the subject when it frames the whole sentence.
The object after 把 should normally be identifiable in context.
The 根据 phrase often stands at the beginning of the sentence but can also follow the subject.
Place the conjunction between parallel words or phrases of the same grammatical role.
These connectors establish an explicit logical relation; later sentence-pattern lessons develop their common pairings.
就 often introduces the result, but it is not required in every sentence.
Put the broader reference and its identifying description next to each other without 的.
This category extends earlier verb-complement phrases with additional complement types introduced at HSK 3.
With 一, common forms include 一个一个 and 一天一天.
The pattern often gives a neutral or mildly positive assessment of something being just right.
就 commonly reinforces the quick transition to the next event.
The expression softens the speaker's commitment to the conclusion.
The complete viewpoint phrase commonly appears before the statement it frames.
Do not add 很 between 越来越 and the adjective.
This expression can soften an assessment by presenting it as an impression.
The phrase often precedes an adjective such as 大, 高兴, or 漂亮.
The expression gives the claim a measured, non-absolute force.
The fixed phrase often sounds less direct than saying something is very bad.
以外 can be omitted when the relationship is clear.
Time expressions are especially common in this pattern.
The frame may begin the sentence or follow the topic being discussed.
The noun or measure phrase after 一 identifies what has a zero count or amount.
The pattern is stronger than a plain 不 before the adjective.
The two clauses may share a subject or use different subjects.
The completed time phrase normally appears before the main action.
Tone and context determine whether the challenge sounds friendly, reassuring, or annoyed.
Depending on context, the pattern can be a reminder, suggestion, or statement of obligation.
Choose 上, 下, or 中 according to the intended relation, not as interchangeable endings.
English often translates the verbal subject with an -ing form or an infinitive.
No nominalizing particle is required between the inner phrase and the main predicate.
Common ordering favors possession or reference before number-classifier phrases and descriptive qualities.
The verb-complement combination should be learned with the result it conventionally expresses.
Common combinations include 看出, 想起, and 写下.
上 often suggests finally starting or getting access to something; 起来 directly highlights the transition into action.
The complements express temporal continuation here, not downward physical direction.
Do not add 了 directly to the potential form to report a completed result.
The pattern can sound more emphatic or assertive than a plain 很 before the adjective.
极了 is broadly evaluative; 坏了 is common with experiences such as being tired, hungry, or anxious.
Compare an ongoing duration such as 学了三年 with 离开家三年了, where the three years begin after leaving.
HSK 3 first lists 怎样 as a question pronoun; this row focuses on its specific placement before a verb.
Depending on tone, it can sound like a friendly reminder or a stronger challenge.
A bare verb is insufficient here; 在 or 到 introduces the required spatial result.
Object 1 is the item or information transferred; Object 2 is its recipient.
Aspect particles and other required material follow the complement according to the completed predicate.
被 often appears with an outcome that significantly affects the patient, whether negative or neutral.
Omitting the agent is natural when it cannot be identified or adds nothing important.
The two verbs share one subject in this serial-verb pattern.
Do not add 很 before the adjective in this comparative pattern.
和 can sometimes replace 跟, but the official target here uses 跟.
Do not insert 很 between 一样 and the adjective.
Use 没有 when directly stating that A has a lower degree; 不比 can leave equality open.
The final quantity measures only the difference between A and B, not A's total amount.
The place is established information; the postverbal entity is the new information being introduced.
Unlike the appearance construction, this pattern presents the postverbal entity as leaving or being removed from the scene.
The frame can contrast the speaker's position with another view, even when that contrast is only implied.
The two verb occurrences must be the same lexical verb and share one subject.
The clauses often share a subject, which can be stated only once.
Keep the two alternatives grammatically parallel for a clear contrast.
The two parts normally describe the same subject or situation.
Adjectives, verb phrases, or other matching predicate types can fill the two slots.
A single 一边 can mark one action's manner, but the paired construction explicitly balances two simultaneous actions.
也 can appear in the 而且 clause when it helps mark the added predicate.
The 可是 clause usually carries greater discourse weight than the 虽然 clause.
The subjects of the two clauses may be the same or different.
This sentence pattern develops the particle 的话 by pairing it systematically with a 就 consequence.
The condition is required, but the pattern does not necessarily claim it is the only factor involved.
Other conditions may also produce the result; the sentence says this one is sufficient.
The purpose clause often comes first to frame the reason for choosing the main action.
The pattern is common for instructions and plans tied to completion of the first event.
This is the approximate-number function of 大概, distinct from its earlier modal use meaning 'probably.'
Do not insert 和 or 或者 between the two numerals.
